summary: Completed testing of Red Tag renaming feature (Warning → Red Tag). Verified user-facing text updated while internal API routes/fields remain unchanged. All features working correctly.
passed_tests: Login page mobile responsive (390x844) - no horizontal scroll
Login page - no default credentials hint (admin123/Default access removed)
Login page - public section with Breakdown and Red Tag buttons present
Red Tag button - yellow theme (#f9f871) with Tag icon
Red Tag button - text says 'RED TAG' not 'Warning'
Login flow works - navigates to Control Room
Public Red Tag kiosk dialog opens (mobile, no login)
Dialog title is 'Report Red Tag'
Dialog has 'RED TAG TYPE' label
Dialog note mentions 'this red tag'
Submit button says 'Raise Red Tag'
No horizontal scroll with dialog open on mobile
Form submission works - creates red tag WRN-00005
Success toast shows 'Red Tag WRN-... raised' message
Breakdowns page - 'Report Red Tag' button present (yellow, Tag icon)
Breakdowns page - view tabs show 'Breakdowns' / 'Red Tags'
Red Tags tab displays warnings table with WRN- numbers
Red tag detail dialog opens with Tag icon
No user-facing 'Warning' word found (except AWS sidebar)
Sidebar 'AWS — Advance Warning' module unchanged (intentional)
Machine drawer - Breakdowns tab has 'Report Red Tag' button
Machine drawer - Timeline filter shows 'red tag created' option
Backend route /api/warnings still works (unchanged)
Backend field names unchanged (tag_number, warning_type, etc.)
Timeline entries show 'Red Tag' wording
Timeline entries show 'WO auto-dispatched from red tag' wording
Internal event_type='warning_created' still used (unchanged)
Error message: 'Red tag work order must be Inspection or Corrective' (400)
Error message: 'Red tag not found' (404)
Analytics endpoint /api/analytics/kpis works (time_utilization, pm_compliance)
Reliability endpoint /api/reliability/metrics works
deploy.sh passes bash -n syntax check
deploy.sh contains systemd service configuration
deploy.sh contains nginx site with /api proxy + WebSocket headers
deploy.sh contains MongoDB repo selection for jammy(7.0)/noble(8.0)
Breakdowns list renders correctly
No critical console errors
